home.metaTitle: Starlights Visuals — Animation, VFX & Game Art Studio
home.metaDescription: A creative studio for 2D / 3D animation, VFX, and game art. We bring fantasy worlds, characters and cinematics to life.
home.heroBody: Starlights Visuals brings stories to life with animation and visual effects that captivate audiences and elevate every frame. We animate fantasy creatures, human characters, vehicles, environments, props, and hard-surface objects—for film, series, advertising, and brand campaigns.

01.title: Animation & VFX
01.desc: Cinematic 2D & 3D animation, character rigs, and visual effects for games and films.

02.title: Game Development
02.desc: Full-cycle production — concept, art, code, audio, ship.

03.title: Concept & Character Art
03.desc: Original characters, creatures, environments, and props that anchor your IP.

04.title: Cinematic Trailers
04.desc: Story-driven trailers built to launch games and franchises.

elena.headline: True professionals who delivered results
elena.quote: The cinematic quality instantly elevated our brand perception. Engagement on our hero campaign jumped within the first week — the visuals feel premium, intentional, and impossible to scroll past.

james.headline: A launch film that felt theatrical
james.quote: Starlights delivered a launch film that felt like a theatrical trailer, not a product ad. Our retention on paid social doubled and the creative became the benchmark for every campaign after.

amara.headline: Surgical craft on a global deadline
amara.quote: From storyboard to final grade, the team was surgical about craft and deadlines. We shipped a global campaign with 2M+ views and a brand lift our leadership still references in board meetings.

servicesPage.metaDescription: 2D animation, 3D animation, game development, character design, motion graphics, VFX, and cinematic trailers.

servicesPage.title: SERVICES
servicesPage.subtitle: One studio, every stage of production — from first sketch to final pixel.
servicesPage.ctaDesc: Tell us your vision — we'll bring the firepower.

2d.title: 2D Animation
2d.desc: Frame-by-frame & rigged 2D for series, shorts, explainers, and games. We blend classic craft with digital pipelines.

motion.desc: Branded motion, UI animation, broadcast packages, and explainer videos with cinematic polish.

vfx.title: Visual Effects (VFX)
vfx.desc: Compositing, particle FX, and CG integration for games, film, and digital campaigns.

trailer.title: Cinematic Trailers
trailer.desc: Story-driven trailers built to launch games, IPs, and brands with maximum impact.

aboutPage.metaDescription: Starlights Visuals is a passionate creative studio specializing in immersive game development, cinematic 2D animation, and high-quality 3D animation experiences.
aboutPage.label: Who We Are
aboutPage.p1: Starlights Visuals is a passionate creative studio at the intersection of games, animation, and cinematic storytelling. We exist to bring imagination to life — whether that's a sprawling open-world game, a hand-crafted 2D series, or a photoreal 3D trailer.
aboutPage.p2: From our concept artists and 3D sculptors to our gameplay engineers and motion designers, every member of our team is obsessed with one thing: making audiences feel something. We don't just build pretty pixels — we build worlds people want to live in.
aboutPage.p3: We partner with indie creators, AAA studios, and global brands to deliver work that punches above its weight, hits deadlines without compromise, and looks like it cost twice the budget.

imagination.title: Imagination First
imagination.desc: Every project starts with a bold idea and a blank canvas.

craft.title: Craft Obsessed
craft.desc: Frame by frame, pixel by pixel — quality is non-negotiable.

energy.title: Cinematic Energy
energy.desc: We chase the goosebumps. If it doesn't move us, we refine.

portfolioPage.label: Selected work

blogPage.metaDescription: Insights on animation, VFX, game art, cinematic trailers, and premium brand storytelling from Starlights Visuals.
blogPage.label: Studio journal
blogPage.subtitle: Thoughts on cinematic craft, production workflows, and building visuals that convert.
blogPage.postBody1: At Starlights Visuals, we treat every frame as a conversion opportunity — not just a beautiful image. This article expands on the thinking behind our {{category}} work and the decisions that separate good creative from launch-ready creative.
blogPage.postBody2: Whether you are shipping a trailer, scaling a brand motion system, or building game art for a live product, the same principle applies: clarity of story first, polish second, and ruthless editing last. That discipline is how teams hit deadlines without the “almost there” feeling.
blogPage.postBody3: Want to apply this thinking to your next project? We would love to hear what you are building — send us a brief and we will map a cinematic path from concept to delivery.

cinematic-trailers-that-convert.title: Why cinematic trailers convert better than product demos
cinematic-trailers-that-convert.excerpt: Story-first launch films build emotional stakes before the CTA. Here is how we structure hooks, pacing, and grade for maximum retention.

character-design-for-ip.title: Character design that anchors your IP across every channel
character-design-for-ip.excerpt: From silhouette readability to merch-ready turnarounds — the checklist we use before a hero goes into production.

game-art-pipeline.title: Inside our game art pipeline: concept to shipped assets
game-art-pipeline.excerpt: How we align art direction, tech constraints, and milestone reviews so teams ship on time without sacrificing polish.

motion-systems-for-brands.title: Building motion systems brands can actually reuse
motion-systems-for-brands.excerpt: Modular motion libraries, logo stings, and UI micro-interactions that keep campaigns consistent at scale.

2d-animation-in-2026.title: 2D animation in 2026: hybrid pipelines that still feel handmade
2d-animation-in-2026.excerpt: Combining rigged efficiency with frame-by-frame accents — when to push each technique for premium results.

creature-design-breakdown.title: Creature design breakdown: from mood board to final rig
creature-design-breakdown.excerpt: A look at how we develop boss creatures with readable silhouettes, material storytelling, and cinematic presence.
